# kcp-commands

> A Claude Code hook that saves 33% of a 200K context window by injecting compact CLI syntax
> before Bash tool calls (Phase A) and filtering noisy output after them (Phase B).
> Phase C writes every tool call to ~/.kcp/events.jsonl for kcp-memory to index.

284 bundled manifests. Java daemon (12ms/call) + Node.js fallback. Apache 2.0.

Part of the [Knowledge Context Protocol](https://github.com/Cantara/knowledge-context-protocol) ecosystem.

## Key files

- [README.md](README.md): Overview, install, how the three phases work, manifest format, releases
- [docs/benchmark-results.md](docs/benchmark-results.md): Full benchmark methodology and measured numbers
- [commands/ps.yaml](commands/ps.yaml): Example manifest showing Phase A + Phase B fields

## Structured Navigation (for AI agents)

This repository ships a KCP manifest at:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Cantara/kcp-commands/main/knowledge.yaml

To use it: fetch the manifest → parse YAML → match query against each unit's `triggers`
(keyword list) and `intent` (natural language) → fetch the `path` of the best match.

Example: "How does Phase B output filtering work?" → trigger `phase b, output filtering` → unit `readme` → fetch README.md

## Install

```bash
# Java daemon (recommended — 12ms/call):
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Cantara/kcp-commands/main/bin/install.sh | bash -s -- --java

# Node.js only (no JVM required):
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Cantara/kcp-commands/main/bin/install.sh | bash -s -- --node
```

## Related

- [kcp-memory](https://github.com/Cantara/kcp-memory) — consumes ~/.kcp/events.jsonl for episodic memory
- [Knowledge Context Protocol](https://github.com/Cantara/knowledge-context-protocol) — the KCP spec
- [Release post](https://wiki.totto.org/blog/2026/03/02/kcp-commands/) — benchmark detail
